page: Vultr voc-g-8c-32gb-160s-amd is an instance within the Optimized Cloud Compute family. It features 8 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC-Rome processor running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The server is configured with 8 physical cores and a 1-to-1 thread-to-core ratio, utilizing a shared CPU allocation model. It includes 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and is equipped with 160 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. No GPU hardware is present.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core tests placing in the top 25% of evaluated systems. Multi-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng and PassMark CPU tests, falls within the average range. Memory bandwidth is also average, with read-only large block speeds at 117.03 GB/sec and write large block speeds at 77.42 GB/sec. In terms of application-level workloads, the server achieves average throughput in Redis SET operations, yielding 15.29 million operations per second, and static web serving at 8.45 GB/sec.

While the server demonstrates strong single-threaded execution, its shared CPU allocation represents a potential tradeoff for sustained multi-threaded workloads. Large-model LLM inference is a notable bottleneck, with prompt processing for a medium 7B model falling into the weak category at 18.38 tokens per second, though smaller model inference remains average. This hardware configuration is suited for general-purpose workloads, moderate database operations, web serving, and lightweight serialization or decompression tasks.
description: Vultr voc-g-8c-32gb-160s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 8 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC-Rome processor with a 2.0 GHz clock speed. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it provides 32.0 GB of RAM and 160 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, with no GPU acceleration. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% in stress-ng tests, while multi-core performance and memory bandwidth remain average. The shared CPU allocation introduces potential resource contention tradeoffs, making it less suitable for heavy multi-threaded tasks or large-scale LLM inference, where prompt processing for 7B models is weak. However, with average performance in Redis operations, static web serving, and database tasks, this server is suited for general-purpose applications, web hosting, and moderate database workloads.
og_description: Vultr voc-g-8c-32gb-160s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance with 8 AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 160 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong single-core and average multi-core performance.
meta_description: Vultr voc-g-8c-32gb-160s-amd features 8 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 160 GB NVMe storage, offering balanced general-purpose performance.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose server featuring AMD EPYC processors, local NVMe storage, and strong single-core performance for standard cloud workloads.
bullet_points: 8 AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs with a 2.0 GHz clock speed
32.0 GB system memory and 160 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Shared CPU allocation model with average multi-core throughput
Suited for general-purpose web serving and moderate database workloads
